perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall be required to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall endeavor to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be required to purchase' to 'shall endeavor to purchase' weakens Premier's obligation, introducing ambiguity. Previously, a failure to purchase the MAOV would be a breach, now it is uncertain whether failure to meet this volume leads to any repercussions or liabilities. This directly contradicts the full force and effect clause within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated Premier to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume (MAOV). The changed text weakens this obligation to 'endeavor to purchase', introducing ambiguity. The original 'shall be required' created a clear obligation that the 'full force and effect' clause could enforce. The changed text creates a scenario where Premier may not meet the MAOV without necessarily breaching the agreement. This directly contradicts the clause stating that all other terms of the Agreement remain in 'full force and effect' as, if failing to meet the MAOV is no longer a breach, the MAOV obligation has effectively been nullified.
